title: Pericallis papyracea
intro: Pericallis papyracea, also known as 'Papyrus Plant', is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Canary Islands. The health benefits of Pericallis papyracea include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pericallis papyracea include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Pericallis papyracea is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the island of La Palma in the Canary Islands. The plant is a succulent herb with a papyraceous (papery) leaf texture, giving it its name. Pericallis papyracea is often grown in gardens for its attractive flowers and unique foliage. The plant's classification is that of a genus within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants.

side_effects: Pericallis papyracea, a plant native to the Canary Islands, may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sap or other chemical compounds.
